How it works

Compress PDF, Images, and Save Space — Fast & Private

Compress pdf files quickly and securely with Compressly — a free, browser-based tool built for speed, privacy, and simplicity. If you're searching for how to compress a pdf or how to compress pdf files without losing quality, Compressly gives you an easy, no-friction solution. Our tool lets you compress pdf, compress jpg, and optimize web images (PNG, WEBP) right in your browser so your files never leave your device.

Many people need to compress pdf file attachments for email, reduce upload times, or shrink archival documents. Compressly focuses on smart compression that balances file size and visual quality. Choose a target quality, preview results, and download instantly. Whether you need to compress pdf for work, school, or sharing photos, our interface makes it simple to compress image files and PDF documents with a single click.

Why use Compressly? First, it runs entirely in your web browser — that means privacy by design: we never upload your files to any server. Second, it supports the formats you use most: JPG, PNG, WEBP, and PDF. Third, compressing is fast because all processing happens locally. If you want to compress jpg or convert a high-resolution PNG into a smaller web-friendly image, select your files, pick a quality, and compress. For PDFs, Compressly compresses embedded images inside PDFs, which often yields significant savings while preserving text and layout.

Best practices: when you compress pdf files for email, aim for 60–80% quality for general use, and 40–60% for heavy compression where file size matters more than print-quality fidelity. If you need to compress pdf for archiving, keep a slightly higher quality. When compressing images, test a few quality settings to find the sweet spot where visual differences are minimal but file size drops substantially.

Compressly is free to use and requires no account. The tool is perfect for users who want to compress pdf files quickly, compress image files before uploading to websites, or reduce storage costs. Because the work is done locally, it also works offline and in constrained environments.

Common tasks our users complete: compress pdf before emailing, compress jpg for web galleries, compress image assets for faster page load times, and batch-compress multiple files at once. If you're wondering how to compress a pdf on desktop or in-browser, Compressly provides a consistent experience across devices without complicated installs.

Get started now: drag and drop a PDF or image onto the tool, choose a quality setting, and click Compress. Preview the before/after file sizes and download your optimized file. Compressly helps you compress pdf and images quickly so you can share, upload, and store files more efficiently.

We care about transparency and user control. Compression settings are visible and reversible — you decide the balance between size and quality. For most users, compressing a pdf or a jpg yields 50–80% smaller files with almost invisible quality loss. Try different settings to find what works best for your needs.

Ready to reduce file size and speed up sharing? Use Compressly to compress pdf, compress jpg, and optimize images — free, fast, and private. If you have questions about how to compress pdf files or which quality setting to choose, check our FAQ above or reach out via the About page.

About Privacy Policy Terms & Conditions Contact

Frequently Asked Questions — Compress PDF & Images

Is Compressly free to use?
Yes — Compressly is completely free to use. There is no signup required, no watermarks, and no hidden fees.
Are my images uploaded to a server?
No. Compression runs locally in your browser using the Canvas API and other browser APIs. Your files are not uploaded to our servers.
What quality setting should I use?
For general web use, 60–80% quality usually offers the best balance between file size and visible quality. For photos, try 70–80%; for aggressive size reduction, try 40–60%.
Can I compress multiple images at once?
Yes — select or drag multiple files into the tool and use the "Compress All" action to batch-process images.
Does PNG compression work the same way?
PNG files are handled differently; Compressly optimizes PNGs and allows converting PNG to JPG when appropriate for better compression.
How to compress a PDF?
Use Compressly by dragging your PDF into the tool, selecting a compression quality, and clicking Compress. The tool re-encodes embedded images to reduce overall file size while preserving text and layout.
How to compress PDF files?
Batch-upload one or more PDFs, pick a quality level, and compress. You can preview before downloading and repeat with a lower quality for smaller sizes.
How to compress a file?
If the file is an image or PDF, use Compressly to optimize it in-browser. For other file types, consider zipping or using specific optimizers for that format.
How to compress a PDF file?
Open Compressly, upload the PDF, adjust the image quality slider, and compress. The result downloads immediately—no uploads to our servers.
How to compress a PDF for free?
Compressly is free: compress PDFs and images entirely in your browser without creating an account. Choose lower quality to achieve smaller sizes.
How to compress PDF file size?
Reduce embedded image quality, remove unnecessary images, or downsample high-resolution images. Compressly focuses on image recompression inside PDFs to reduce file size effectively.
How to compress an image?
Upload JPG, PNG, or WEBP, choose a quality percentage, and compress. For photos, 60–80% often provides good balance between size and quality.
How to compress a PDF on Mac?
On macOS, you can use Preview > Export to create a smaller file, or use Compressly in your browser for more control over image quality and batch processing.
Compress PDF to 100KB / 200KB / 500KB / 1MB
Target sizes depend on input content. To reach small targets like 100KB, reduce image quality and remove pages/images. Try multiple passes with lower quality settings; extremely small targets may sacrifice visible quality.
iLovePDF compress PDF — comparison
iLovePDF is a popular online service that also compresses PDFs. Unlike some services, Compressly processes files locally in your browser for improved privacy—choose the tool that matches your privacy needs.
Free compress PDF / Compress PDF to 1MB free
Compressly offers free, in-browser compression that can often reach targets like 1MB depending on the original file. Experiment with quality settings to balance size and readability.
